name: Action Alert (via Van Hollen HQ)
subject: It's even meaner...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| **BREAKING:** Republican Leader Mitch McConnell FINALLY released the secret TrumpCare bill that he had been hiding from Democratic Senators, members of his caucus and the American people.

In many ways, this bill is even meaner than the one the House passed last month.

**[We need 3 Republicans to defeat this horrific bill. Join Chris to make sure every Republican Senator knows that the American people will hold them accountable for what happens next. Add your name now>>](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bc6/70cdc2ba/47769d15/397c736/334954829/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/sign-help-us-defeat-the-republican-health-care-bill?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+We+need+3+Republicans+to+defeat+this+hor&utm_campaign=em062117&source=em062117")**
---

Mitch McConnell knew that when the details of the Republican Trumpcare bill were made public, he would have to deal with an immediate backlash. That's why he was working to draft this bill in total secrecy behind closed doors.

Today he went public. But the Republican leader has no intention of allowing real debate and amendments on this legislation. He plans to ram it through the Senate next week before the July 4 recess.

This bill is nothing more than a tax cut for the wealthy masquerading as a 'health care' bill. It will reduce access to care for millions of Americans, particularly the elderly, women, and those with pre-existing conditions -- many of whom are at risk of losing their healthcare all together.

[**Now is not the time to stay silent - we must fight this reckless plan and find THREE Senate Republicans who will side with the AMERICAN PEOPLE on this one. Add your name now >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bc6/70cdc2ba/47769d15/397c737/334954829/VEsE/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/sign-help-us-defeat-the-republican-health-care-bill?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Now+is+not+the+time+to+stay+silent++we+m&utm_campaign=em062117&source=em062117")

On the Senate floor today Mitch McConnell actually stated that "American families deserve better health care." We couldn't agree more. But the bill he is pushing would do the opposite -- it will deny millions of Americans a vital lifeline to affordable care by slashing Medicaid, gutting funding for preventive care for women, and threatening care for the most vulnerable and those with pre-existing conditions. And it will do this while it provides MASSIVE tax cuts to the wealthiest.

This is simply too important to ignore.

[**Add your name to join Chris in this fight and stand up for the health of the American people.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bc6/70cdc2ba/47769d15/397c734/334954829/VEsF/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/sign-help-us-defeat-the-republican-health-care-bill?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+Add+your+name+to+join+Chris+in+this+figh&utm_campaign=em062117&source=em062117")

We can do better than this. We must.

Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
---

[www.vanhollen.org/donate](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bc6/70cdc2ba/47769d15/397c735/334954829/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/vanhollen?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=4+-+wwwvanhollenorgdonate&utm_campaign=em062117&source=em062117")

We believe that emails are a vital way to stay in direct contact with supporters. You're receiving this email at dpwillis67@gmail.com. [**Click here**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bc6/70cdc2ba/47769d15/397c732/334954829/VEsD/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=5+-+Click+here&utm_campaign=em062117&source=em062117") if you'd like to unsubscribe from these messages. Please enter dpwillis67@gmail.com to ensure that we are able to unsubscribe you from our list.

name: Health Care Update (via DSCC)
subject: It's no wonder the GOP tried to hide this bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
With a vote likely next week, we have to turn up the pressure on Republicans to drop their Obamacare repeal bill.
---
**[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170622-HealthCareUpdate_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Team --

We're reading through the GOP health care bill, and it's a total disaster. There is no other way to put this: if there's a chance you might get sick, get old, or have a family, this bill could impact you.

The GOP plan raises costs, reduces coverage, rolls back protections, and destroys Medicaid as we know it. It's not a health care plan at all.

[**With a vote likely next week, we have to turn up the pressure on Republicans to drop this bill. Chip in $1 right now to help fund our ads and stop Republicans from destroying Obamacare.**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170622-HealthCareUpdate_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

What's perhaps most despicable about this bill -- yes, even worse than what we've already mentioned -- are the massive tax cuts to the wealthiest Americans, at the expense of programs like Medicaid that help middle-class and poor families.

It's no wonder that Republicans tried so hard to hide this bill from the public. They should be ashamed for backing a bill that would strip health care from millions of Americans to give billionaires a tax break -- and we're doing everything in our power to call them out before the vote.

**Senate Democrats are fighting as hard as they can to stop the repeal bill and they urgently need backup from us before the vote. Chip in $1 right now to the fight to save Obamacare:

[http://dscc.org/fight-to-save-obamacare](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170622-HealthCareUpdate_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**

Right now, we are not only fighting for healthcare, but for who we are as a country - whether we'll take care of each other or leave our fellow Americans to fend for themselves. This is a fight for our values, and we need your support now.

Thank you,

Team DSCC

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: Tom Perez
subject: We've seen the bill. Here's how we're fighting back:
body:
We need your help to save affordable health care.
---
**We've seen the Senate GOP's secret bill, so we launched a new ad to hold Republicans accountable on health care. Chip in $3 or whatever you can to help us keep it on the air and keep getting the word out about the GOP's health care repeal:
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052331b/4067b0a/34b8968a2/7e680a5b/489237338/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Health-Care-Ad-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170623_tp_nd&source=em_20170623_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170623_tp_nd")****
Derek, I can't say for certain when Republicans decided to stop caring about the needs of the American people. But from the way they've gone about repealing our health care, it's clear that they have.

On the one hand, you've got the House Republicans who were so proud of themselves for voting to repeal the Affordable Care Act that they rushed to Donald Trump's side to celebrate taking health coverage away from 23 million Americans. And on the other, there are Senate Republicans who hid what they were doing because they know the consequences will be disastrous for their constituents.

We're going to make sure the American people know the truth, Derek. Take a look at the new ad we launched to help get the word out:

[](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052331b/4067b0a/34b8968a2/7e680a58/489237338/VEsE/ "http://my.democrats.org/Health-Care-Ad-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=em_20170623_tp_nd&source=em_20170623_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170623_tp_nd")
There are lives at stake if the GOP's health care repeal becomes law. That's why we have to make sure the American people know what Republicans are trying to do and have the tools to make their voices heard to stop this bill.

Pitch in $3 or whatever you can to help us spread this very important message all over the country and hold the GOP's feet to the fire -- before it's too late.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

clean_body: "" "" Bumping this email, because this is important -- Senate Republicans' version of Trumpcare gives enormous tax breaks to corporate special interests, all while defunding Planned Parenthood and undermining protections for people with pre-existing conditions. And, it guts Medicaid -- a program that helps older Americans, pregnant women, and those living with disabilities get the care they need. Mitch McConnell knows that hard-working Americans like you will be hit hard by what 's in the bill, so he's trying to push it through as quickly as he can, without giving you a chance to read it. There 's even talk about bringing it to a vote next week. "" Thank you, Team Maggie \-----------Original Message----------- On Thursday, June 22 at 3:26 PM, Team Maggie <info@maggiehassan.com> wrote: Team, we just got a first look at the bill Senate Republicans have been working on in secret, and it's even worse than we expected. It defunds Planned Parenthood, makes even more cuts to Medicaid than the House bill, and undermines essential health benefits including maternity and prescription drug coverage. In short, it would rip lifesaving care away from millions of our families, friends, and neighbors. "" We won't mince words: this bill would discriminate against women by allowing insurance companies to charge them more for their care, it would drive up premiums and out of pocket costs, and it would unfairly tax older Americans. We can still stop this bill from passing the Senate, but only if each and every one of us step up to the plate. Team, the time to act is NOW. "" Lives are on the line here, everyone, so thank you for taking action. -- Team Maggie ""

name: Bobby Jindal
subject: How about we actually repeal it?
body:
[](http://wc4.net/t?r=4673&c=920&l=203&ctl=2D4C:C15079EF2606CA7C9A4EA75FA8382ED0D2A4146F44E9B90D&)
---
**Making the House Health Bill More Conservative**
By Bobby Jindal
President Trump has finally achieved unity and consensus in our nation’s capital. Pundits, Republican and Democrat members of Congress, and the media all seem to agree that everything must stop so that we can endlessly speculate on Russia, special counsels, the FBI, the last election, and criminal charges. Nonsense. Voters elected President Trump and the Republican majorities in Congress to drain the swamp, i.e., to make big and fundamental changes in the direction of our country. Let Mueller, and the appropriate Congressional committees, talk to Comey, Flynn, and even Putin if they want, but in the meantime, do not let Democrats run out the clock. Our country faces too many dire challenges, thanks in large part to the Obama years, that must be addressed now. Congress is, despite its earned reputation, actually capable of walking and chewing gum at the same time. I have a radical idea – why don’t our political leaders do what they promised when they asked for our votes just a few months ago? They could start by actually repealing and replacing Obamacare.

Now that the House has finally passed its health care legislation, attention turns toward the Senate. House leadership claimed certain popular provisions could not be added, e.g., allowing the sale of insurance across state lines, and other unpopular provisions could not be removed, e.g., the special treatment for Members of Congress and their staff, to ensure rapid Senate approval of the House legislation. Despite these assurances and compromises, Republican Senators are now talking about writing their own legislation. While the House compromise that allows states the option to apply for a federal waiver to repeal many of Obamacare’s regulations disappointed conservatives wanting a full repeal, early indications from the Senate are their changes will be to make the legislation less and not more conservative. (Indeed, why not at least require states to apply to keep, rather than to replace, Obamacare’s regulations?)

Senate Republicans should adopt as their guideposts the conservative goals of reducing federal spending, dependence on subsidies, and government intrusion. President Obama’s unprecedented Medicaid expansion to cover millions of able bodied adults violates all three. It wasn’t that long ago when Republicans were in near unanimous agreement that reducing welfare rolls, through policies like time limits and work requirements, was a positive thing. A safety net should be provided for the neediest amongst us, but it should be temporary, targeted, and locally governed.

Senate Republicans should strengthen the House bill’s provisions by giving states even more flexibility to design and run their Medicaid programs. Multi-year federal grants for states should be tied to some measures of eligibility, but not simply to enrollment. Just as states currently benefit when they reduce their welfare rolls, they should not be rewarded for keeping people on Medicaid longer than necessary or punished for encouraging individuals to afford private coverage. States should also have the flexibility and financial incentives to design wrap-around coverage or premium assistance plans rather than one size fits all benefits packages that often crowd out private coverage. Current rules technically allow but actually discourage states from pursuing such policies. A beneficiary with employer provided or individual coverage might require targeted assistance, for example, affording prescription drugs or cost sharing, but should not have to choose between keeping their private coverage and getting help.

Just as the House bill gives states the option to manage their own individual insurance marketplaces, as they did before Obamacare, so should the Senate give states the ability to manage their own Medicaid programs. States should no longer have to come begging to Washington to design benefit packages, cost sharing provisions, or eligibility requirements that meet the unique needs of their constituents. Washington can and should require accountability through overall performance measures, to ensure states are focused on improving health outcomes and not just complying with complicated rules, and special protections for the most vulnerable populations, e.g., the elderly and disabled.

A second conservative change would be for the Senate to transform the House’s refundable tax credit into a tax deduction. This would have to be accompanied by funding for state designed programs to assist needy individuals who do not earn enough income to pay taxes and benefit from the deduction, but at least this subsidy would be explicitly identified as spending and could thus be managed, rather than hiding a new federal entitlement program in an increasingly complicated tax code. A deduction would incentivize consumers to buy health care more efficiently, as they would keep the savings, and insurers and providers to compete to reduce costs. Independent scoring of this provision previously has shown it to be one of the more effective ways to “bend the cost curve down.”

Conservatives have waited many years to repeal and replace Obamacare. Perhaps it is too ambitious to hope the Senate improves the House legislation, rather than merely playing defense and trying to minimize the ways they will make it worse. However, President Obama himself campaigned on the promise to be transformational and not merely incremental – surely, we should aim no lower.

* * *

Nonsense. Voters elected President Trump and the Republican majorities in Congress to drain the swamp, i.e., to make big and fundamental changes in the direction of our country. Let Mueller, and the appropriate Congressional committees, talk to Comey, Flynn, and even Putin if they want, but in the meantime, do not let Democrats run out the clock. Our country faces too many dire challenges, thanks in large part to the Obama years, that must be addressed now. Congress is, despite its earned reputation, actually capable of walking and chewing gum at the same time. I have a radical idea – why don’t our political leaders do what they promised when they asked for our votes just a few months ago? They could start by actually repealing and replacing Obamacare. Now that the House has finally passed its health care legislation, attention turns toward the Senate. House leadership claimed certain popular provisions could not be added, e.g., allowing the sale of insurance across state lines, and other unpopular provisions could not be removed, e.g., the special treatment for Members of Congress and their staff, to ensure rapid Senate approval of the House legislation. Despite these assurances and compromises, Republican Senators are now talking about writing their own legislation. While the House compromise that allows states the option to apply for a federal waiver to repeal many of Obamacare’s regulations disappointed conservatives wanting a full repeal, early indications from the Senate are their changes will be to make the legislation less and not more conservative. (Indeed, why not at least require states to apply to keep, rather than to replace, Obamacare’s regulations?) Senate Republicans should adopt as their guideposts the conservative goals of reducing federal spending, dependence on subsidies, and government intrusion. President Obama’s unprecedented Medicaid expansion to cover millions of able bodied adults violates all three. It wasn’t that long ago when Republicans were in near unanimous agreement that reducing welfare rolls, through policies like time limits and work requirements, was a positive thing. A safety net should be provided for the neediest amongst us, but it should be temporary, targeted, and locally governed. Senate Republicans should strengthen the House bill’s provisions by giving states even more flexibility to design and run their Medicaid programs. Multi-year federal grants for states should be tied to some measures of eligibility, but not simply to enrollment. Just as states currently benefit when they reduce their welfare rolls, they should not be rewarded for keeping people on Medicaid longer than necessary or punished for encouraging individuals to afford private coverage. States should also have the flexibility and financial incentives to design wrap-around coverage or premium assistance plans rather than one size fits all benefits packages that often crowd out private coverage. Current rules technically allow but actually discourage states from pursuing such policies. A beneficiary with employer provided or individual coverage might require targeted assistance, for example, affording prescription drugs or cost sharing, but should not have to choose between keeping their private coverage and getting help. Just as the House bill gives states the option to manage their own individual insurance marketplaces, as they did before Obamacare, so should the Senate give states the ability to manage their own Medicaid programs. States should no longer have to come begging to Washington to design benefit packages, cost sharing provisions, or eligibility requirements that meet the unique needs of their constituents. Washington can and should require accountability through overall performance measures, to ensure states are focused on improving health outcomes and not just complying with complicated rules, and special protections for the most vulnerable populations, e.g., the elderly and disabled. A second conservative change would be for the Senate to transform the House’s refundable tax credit into a tax deduction. This would have to be accompanied by funding for state designed programs to assist needy individuals who do not earn enough income to pay taxes and benefit from the deduction, but at least this subsidy would be explicitly identified as spending and could thus be managed, rather than hiding a new federal entitlement program in an increasingly complicated tax code. A deduction would incentivize consumers to buy health care more efficiently, as they would keep the savings, and insurers and providers to compete to reduce costs. Independent scoring of this provision previously has shown it to be one of the more effective ways to “bend the cost curve down.” Conservatives have waited many years to repeal and replace Obamacare. Perhaps it is too ambitious to hope the Senate improves the House legislation, rather than merely playing defense and trying to minimize the ways they will make it worse. However, President Obama himself campaigned on the promise to be transformational and not merely incremental – surely, we should aim no lower. *

The Senate just doubled down on Trumpcare. Now we have to team up to stop it.

The Senate version of Trumpcare released yesterday, after weeks of secret talks, is just the latest example of their unconscionable attacks on working people. The elderly will see a new age tax imposed. Millions with preexisting conditions will be left without coverage. Americans with autism and disabilities will be hurt and left in jeopardy. And the all-male panel who wrote this bill are leaving women across America without access to reproductive health. The list goes on but make no mistake: This version of Trumpcare is a cruel and cynical bill. Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell have crafted a massive tax cut for their billionaire friends and they're funding it on the backs of working people and those who desperately need health care. It's wrong. It's mean. And it shouldn't surprise us. This moment demands a state-by-state movement to stop Trumpcare. I need your help, Washington state needs your help, and America needs your help! | | Peter,

After weeks of secret negotiations, Senate Republicans just released the text of their bill to repeal the Affordable Care Act, and frankly, it's heartbreaking.

Among other things, this bill would:
* Gut Medicaid, which covers about half of all births in the United States;
* Cause seniors' premiums to skyrocket;
* Put addiction treatment programs out of reach for the people who need them most.

Babies. Seniors. Moms and dads -- these are the everyday folks I came to the Senate to fight for, and a plan that decimates their coverage to fund tax breaks for the ultra-rich will _never, ever_ get my vote.

I am willing to work with anyone who has good ideas for how to lower premiums, expand coverage, and improve the ACA -- but this isn't the way.

Peter, the Republicans are already campaigning to replace me with someone who will be a rubber stamp for their heartless slash-and-burn agenda, and I need your help.

[**Can you chip in $5 to my campaign so that I can stay in the Senate fighting for you?**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c26e5/7377f838/3680c381/51fb1df7/17445812/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-hip?refcode=20170623nd&amount=10")

The GOP's methodical campaign to sabotage the Affordable Care Act is already having real consequences for people in Indiana. On Wednesday, two of the biggest insurance providers in the state announced that they were pulling out of our exchange because of the volatility and uncertainty that Congressional Republicans and the Trump Administration have intentionally created.

Now, they're trying to rush through this terrible bill without so much as a single committee hearing and inflict even more damage.

**Being a Democrat from a conservative state who is fighting the GOP 's repeal efforts tooth and nail has put a huge target on my back. I'm okay with that -- but I need your help to defend against the barrage of attacks.**

[**Let 's fight this together: Chip in $5 to my campaign right now so I can stay in the Senate and keep fighting for Indiana.**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c26e5/7377f838/3680c381/51fb1df7/17445812/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-hip?refcode=20170623nd&amount=10")

**_VP Pence Comes to Town_**
We Hoosiers are a lucky bunch. In 2016, we not only had the eyes of the nation on our state when we officially put **Donald Trump** over the top in the race for the Republican nomination for president, but we also got to witness history when our very own **Mike Pence** was elected vice president of the United States! And now, when the vice president comes to visit, it's not just a big deal, it's a homecoming!

I was honored to have the opportunity to introduce Vice President Pence and Second Lady **Karen Pence** at an event last Friday night in Indianapolis. As I said that night, Mike Pence is not your typical vice president. He has been tasked with important roles both in Washington and around the globe. In fact, he's working hard on both sides of Pennsylvania Avenue. On one end, he’s in the room, at the table, advising the president and helping to lead this Administration on the issues Americans care about the most. On the other end, he’s in constant communication with members of Congress, utilizing the relationships he built over more than a decade in the House of Representatives to advance the president’s priorities. And, he's been dispatched more than once to travel overseas to meet with allies during these challenging times.

I'm very proud of the work our vice president is doing and look forward to many, many more visits to the Hoosier State in the months to come!

\- Kyle

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer with Second Lady Karen Pence and Vice President Mike Pence at the Great America Committee event in Indianapolis on Friday, June 16, 2017.)

**_Joe Donnelly's Health Care Amnesia_**
Let's set the scene: Joe Donnelly, who voted for the job-killing, premium-hiking, chaos-creating Affordable Care Act (a/k/a Obamacare) [sauntered onto the floor of the United States Senate](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f318c0d709&e=1acd9bb3d6) Monday afternoon to pin the failures of his beloved bill on...wait for it...President Donald Trump. You literally cannot make this up. Is amnesia covered under Obamacare?

Our state’s senior senator simply cannot bring himself to admit that the bill he voted for at the behest of Barack Obama and Nancy Pelosi has brought so much pain and uncertainty to Hoosiers and the American people. So, he did what a politician like him would do, he turned up the rhetoric and re-assigned blame to the other political party.

THEN, two days later, Anthem and MDWise brought Senator Donnelly another [Obamacare-induced headache](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9ff326b904&e=1acd9bb3d6) when they became the latest insurers to flee Indiana’s Obamacare exchange leaving just two – yes, two – insurers left.

THEN, hours later, Tone-Deaf Donnelly [blasted out a fundraising email](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a89fe9d143&e=1acd9bb3d6) applauding the successes of Obamacare and begging folks to send him cash so he can keep defending the indefensible.

If Joe Donnelly is looking for someone to blame for Obamacare's failures...he should look in the mirror.

clean_body: | | | | | | | | VP Pence Comes to Town We Hoosiers are a lucky bunch. In 2016, we not only had the eyes of the nation on our state when we officially put Donald Trump over the top in the race for the Republican nomination for president, but we also got to witness history when our very own Mike Pence was elected vice president of the United States! And now, when the vice president comes to visit, it's not just a big deal, it's a homecoming! I was honored to have the opportunity to introduce Vice President Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence at an event last Friday night in Indianapolis. As I said that night, Mike Pence is not your typical vice president. He has been tasked with important roles both in Washington and around the globe. In fact, he's working hard on both sides of Pennsylvania Avenue. On one end, he’s in the room, at the table, advising the president and helping to lead this Administration on the issues Americans care about the most. On the other end, he’s in constant communication with members of Congress, utilizing the relationships he built over more than a decade in the House of Representatives to advance the president’s priorities. And, he's been dispatched more than once to travel overseas to meet with allies during these challenging times. I'm very proud of the work our vice president is doing and look forward to many, many more visits to the Hoosier State in the months to come! \- Kyle (Photo: Chairman Hupfer with Second Lady Karen Pence and Vice President Mike Pence at the Great America Committee event in Indianapolis on Friday, June 16, 2017.) | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Governor Eric Holcomb concluded his first overseas economic development mission as our state's chief executive this week. The trip was full of meetings, making new friends, strengthening existing relationships and some game-changing announcements. Here are a few of the stories about the Governor's trip: IBJ: IEDC Release: WFYI: Inside Indiana Business: | | Joe Donnelly's Health Care Amnesia Let's set the scene: Joe Donnelly, who voted for the job-killing, premium-hiking, chaos-creating Affordable Care Act (a/k/a Obamacare) Monday afternoon to pin the failures of his beloved bill on...wait for it...President Donald Trump. You literally cannot make this up. Is amnesia covered under Obamacare? Our state’s senior senator simply cannot bring himself to admit that the bill he voted for at the behest of Barack Obama and Nancy Pelosi has brought so much pain and uncertainty to Hoosiers and the American people. So, he did what a politician like him would do, he turned up the rhetoric and re-assigned blame to the other political party. THEN, two days later, Anthem and MDWise brought Senator Donnelly another when they became the latest insurers to flee Indiana’s Obamacare exchange leaving just two – yes, two – insurers left. THEN, hours later, Tone-Deaf Donnelly applauding the successes of Obamacare and begging folks to send him cash so he can keep defending the indefensible. If Joe Donnelly is looking for someone to blame for Obamacare's failures...he should look in the mirror. | | News You Can Use Lt. Gov. Senate Republicans kept their health care plan secret for weeks. Now we know why.The plan they unveiled yesterday takes us backward. Their plan especially hurts those who can least afford it -- children, seniors, and the disabled.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202c84/2fde7c66/42971330/15697e71/1941655798/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/vote-no-ahca?firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em17_170623_ahca_adv_gq_ful__rc&source=em17_170623_ahca_adv_gq_ful__rc")

Friends,

Senate Republicans kept their health care plan secret for weeks. Now we know why.

The plan they unveiled yesterday takes us backward. Their plan especially hurts those who can least afford it -- children, seniors, and the disabled.

Health care should be affordable and accessible to all.

Unfortunately, this new Senate plan (like the House bill before it) does the opposite.

**[I'm calling on North Carolina's senators -- Senator Burr and Senator Tillis -- to vote NO on the bill unveiled yesterday. Add your name here to join me.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202c84/2fde7c66/42971330/15697e70/1941655798/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/vote-no-ahca?firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=2+-+Im+calling+on+North+Carolinas+senators++&utm_campaign=em17_170623_ahca_adv_gq_ful__rc&source=em17_170623_ahca_adv_gq_ful__rc")**

In government, it's our job to work together to come up with lasting solutions to real problems.

Right now, a serious problem facing communities in North Carolina and across the country is opioid addiction.

As I said at last week's White House Opioid Commission meeting, taking away health care from millions of people only hurts our battle against the opioid crisis.

**[This bill is wrong for our state and wrong for the country. Urge our senators to vote NO.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202c84/2fde7c66/42971330/15697e73/1941655798/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/vote-no-ahca?firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=3+-+This+bill+is+wrong+for+our+state+and+wro&utm_campaign=em17_170623_ahca_adv_gq_ful__rc&source=em17_170623_ahca_adv_gq_ful__rc")**

Thanks for all you do,

Roy

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, June 23rd |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
It’s been a great week! Not only here in New Hampshire, but across the country. Whether it’s congressional seats in South Carolina or Georgia, or record-breaking fundraising efforts by the Republican National Committee or here in New Hampshire with the passage of a bi-partisan fiscally responsible budget. Republicans keep winning!
The FY18/19 budget has total spending of $11.7 billion with no new taxes or fees. This budget promotes job creation through small business tax cuts and also lowers energy costs with the elimination of the energy consumption tax. The budget also grows our rainy day fund to $100.7 million - - which means that this will protect New Hampshire’s bond rating and strengthen our financial position.
The budget focuses important resources on the opioid crisis, mental health, and prioritizes families with disabled children. It overhauls the failing Division of Children, Youth, and Families and also makes major investments in NH students by funding $5ml in student scholarships.
This budget reflects the strong tradition of sound, conservative fiscal management that Granite Staters have come to expect from the Republican Party.
Many thanks to the Governor, the Senate, and the House for their hard work on the budget. Thanks also to the House Republican Alliance and Freedom Caucus for their feedback in the process. Last, but not least, thanks to Democrats who showed courage to cross over and vote for a budget that serves the people of New Hampshire well.
It feels good to win...it feels even better that we keep winning!
Sincerely, _Jeanie_

| **Legislative Highlights** |
---|---|---
---
| _**House Republicans**_
|
---
| **CONCORD** – House Republican Leaders issued statements following the affirmative vote of the State Senate and House of Representatives to send HB144 and HB517 to the governor’s desk for signature. The committee of conference report on HB144 received a 198-169 vote, and HB517 received a 212-161 vote on Thursday, after months of public input, review, and analysis by House and Senate budget writers.
**House Speaker Shawn Jasper (R-Hudson):**
“This is a budget the legislature and the people of New Hampshire can be proud of. This budget provides increased resources to address the opioid crisis, mental illness, domestic violence, and includes several reforms to state government while keeping spending in check. We’ve achieved a balance that ensures our citizens will have access to the services they need while reducing the tax burden. Members of the House and Senate have worked tirelessly on this budget, which in my opinion makes sense for our state, our communities, and our constituents.”
**House Majority Leader Dick Hinch (R-Merrimack):**
“Voters sent Republican majorities back to Concord to continue managing our state in a fiscally responsible manner. This budget meets the needs of our state and addresses many of our top priorities. New Hampshire will be more competitive by reducing the tax burden on our business community and reducing electricity bills by repealing a tax on electricity consumption. Republicans united today to keep promises made during the campaign, and we delivered.”
**House Finance Committee Chairman Neal Kurk (R-Weare):**
“This is a thoughtful and conservative budget that provides essential services for our state’s most vulnerable populations without raising taxes or fees. We worked closely with the Senate to deliver this compassionate and fiscally responsible spending plan for our state. This was an inclusive process from start to finish, and this budget reflects our desire to meet the demands of our constituents and build consensus within the House and Senate.” |
---
---
---
| _**Senate Republicans**_
**Morse, Daniels praise passage of NH budget** Concord, NH - Today, the House and Senate passed the budget committee of conference reports on HB 144 and HB 517. “I am very pleased that today the House and Senate were able to come together and unite through a strong vote for passage of a budget that takes care of our neediest citizens, continues to grow New Hampshire’s economy and creates jobs all while spending only what the state can afford,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “Building upon the momentum to grow our state’s growing economy, this budget further reduces the tax burden on small businesses, doubles the amount of funding to support those battling substance abuse, targets funding to address mental health needs, increases funding for families with a disabled child and overhauls DCYF to protect our children,” added Morse. “The work we’ve done to address critical issues facing our citizens and our state’s job creators will undoubtedly benefit New Hampshire well into the future and I thank my colleagues in the House and Senate for their ability to come together to pass a budget today that spends only what the state can afford,” said Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford). “This budget also focuses on expanding care for those with mental health needs and restructuring the Division of Children Youth and Families to better support our state’s children in this budget.” “In a stark contrast to Massachusetts who had recently had their bond rating lowered, we’re also putting New Hampshire on a strong financial footing by growing our state’s Rainy Day Fund from a low of $9 million to more than $100 million through this budget,” said Daniels. “We’ve worked diligently to bring everyone’s voices to the table during the budget process and I am proud to have the support of the legislature and the Governor and look forward to his signature.” |

clean_body: | | | NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, June 23rd | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, It’s been a great week! Not only here in New Hampshire, but across the country. Whether it’s congressional seats in South Carolina or Georgia, or record-breaking fundraising efforts by the Republican National Committee or here in New Hampshire with the passage of a bi-partisan fiscally responsible budget. Republicans keep winning! The FY18/19 budget has total spending of $11.7 billion with no new taxes or fees. This budget promotes job creation through small business tax cuts and also lowers energy costs with the elimination of the energy consumption tax. The budget also grows our rainy day fund to $100.7 million - - which means that this will protect New Hampshire’s bond rating and strengthen our financial position. The budget focuses important resources on the opioid crisis, mental health, and prioritizes families with disabled children. It overhauls the failing Division of Children, Youth, and Families and also makes major investments in NH students by funding $5ml in student scholarships. This budget reflects the strong tradition of sound, conservative fiscal management that Granite Staters have come to expect from the Republican Party. Many thanks to the Governor, the Senate, and the House for their hard work on the budget. Thanks also to the House Republican Alliance and Freedom Caucus for their feedback in the process. Last, but not least, thanks to Democrats who showed courage to cross over and vote for a budget that serves the people of New Hampshire well. It feels good to win...it feels even better that we keep winning! Sincerely, Jeanie | | | Join the Grassroots Circle! | | The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. | | | | Intern with the NH GOP! | | Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. | | | Featured Event | ---|---|--- | Upcoming Events | ---|---|--- | NHYR Canvass and Cookout June 24 10am-4pm NHGOP HQ, 10 Water Street, Concord, NH Bedford GOP Summer Social June 28 - 5:30 PM Riddle Brook School, 230 New Boston Road, Bedford, NH | | | | | | Photo of the Week | ---|---|--- Thursday, State House and Senate Republicans join Governor Chris Sununu to celebrate the passage of the 2018-2019 New Hampshire Budget. | Republican Spotlight! | ---|---|--- | Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight shines on the Concord GOP! The Concord GOP has been always been there to help the party and lead the way forward for the Republican State Committee. They have been busy recruiting volunteers to help with the upcoming House Special Election in Concord, Ward 9 for Republican Michael Feeley. The Concord GOP is currently set up at Concord Market Days and their booth will be there all day tomorrow! Stop by to say hello to the committee members and grab some great lemonade! Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans | | CONCORD – House Republican Leaders issued statements following the affirmative vote of the State Senate and House of Representatives to send HB144 and HB517 to the governor’s desk for signature. The committee of conference report on HB144 received a 198-169 vote, and HB517 received a 212-161 vote on Thursday, after months of public input, review, and analysis by House and Senate budget writers. House Speaker Shawn Jasper (R-Hudson): “This is a budget the legislature and the people of New Hampshire can be proud of. This budget provides increased resources to address the opioid crisis, mental illness, domestic violence, and includes several reforms to state government while keeping spending in check. We’ve achieved a balance that ensures our citizens will have access to the services they need while reducing the tax burden. Members of the House and Senate have worked tirelessly on this budget, which in my opinion makes sense for our state, our communities, and our constituents.” House Majority Leader Dick Hinch (R-Merrimack): “Voters sent Republican majorities back to Concord to continue managing our state in a fiscally responsible manner. This budget meets the needs of our state and addresses many of our top priorities. New Hampshire will be more competitive by reducing the tax burden on our business community and reducing electricity bills by repealing a tax on electricity consumption. Republicans united today to keep promises made during the campaign, and we delivered.” House Finance Committee Chairman Neal Kurk (R-Weare): “This is a thoughtful and conservative budget that provides essential services for our state’s most vulnerable populations without raising taxes or fees. We worked closely with the Senate to deliver this compassionate and fiscally responsible spending plan for our state. This was an inclusive process from start to finish, and this budget reflects our desire to meet the demands of our constituents and build consensus within the House and Senate.” | | Senate Republicans Morse, Daniels praise passage of NH budget Concord, NH - Today, the House and Senate passed the budget committee of conference reports on HB 144 and HB 517. “I am very pleased that today the House and Senate were able to come together and unite through a strong vote for passage of a budget that takes care of our neediest citizens, continues to grow New Hampshire’s economy and creates jobs all while spending only what the state can afford,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “Building upon the momentum to grow our state’s growing economy, this budget further reduces the tax burden on small businesses, doubles the amount of funding to support those battling substance abuse, targets funding to address mental health needs, increases funding for families with a disabled child and overhauls DCYF to protect our children,” added Morse. “The work we’ve done to address critical issues facing our citizens and our state’s job creators will undoubtedly benefit New Hampshire well into the future and I thank my colleagues in the House and Senate for their ability to come together to pass a budget today that spends only what the state can afford,” said Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford). “This budget also focuses on expanding care for those with mental health needs and restructuring the Division of Children Youth and Families to better support our state’s children in this budget.” “In a stark contrast to Massachusetts who had recently had their bond rating lowered, we’re also putting New Hampshire on a strong financial footing by growing our state’s Rainy Day Fund from a low of $9 million to more than $100 million through this budget,” said Daniels. “We’ve worked diligently to bring everyone’s voices to the table during the budget process and I am proud to have the support of the legislature and the Governor and look forward to his signature.” | | Meet Sen. body:
Did you see what Barack Obama said yesterday about the Senate GOP's health care repeal bill?
---
**[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170623-DidYouSee_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Team,

Did you see what Barack Obama said yesterday about the GOP health care repeal bill? He said:

> _" The Senate bill, unveiled today, is not a health care bill. It's a massive transfer of wealth from middle-class and poor families to the richest people in America. It hands enormous tax cuts to the rich and to the drug and insurance industries, paid for by cutting health care for everybody else. Those with private insurance will experience higher premiums and higher deductibles, with lower tax credits to help working families cover the costs, even as their plans might no longer cover pregnancy, mental health care, or expensive prescriptions. Discrimination based on pre-existing conditions could become the norm again. Millions of families will lose coverage entirely._

_**"Simply put, if there 's a chance you might get sick, get old, or start a family -- this bill will do you harm."**_
Then, he called on all of us to "speak out" and make sure Republican senators know what impact this bill will have on our families.

[**We 're running ads in key states showing what this bill will cost working Americans and with a vote likely next week, we urgently need your help to up the pressure. Chip in $1 right now to help call out the Republican plan and stop this disastrous repeal bill.**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170623-DidYouSee_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

When President Obama left office, he said he'd weigh in on politics only when the stakes were truly high and the moment demanded he speak out.

This is one of those moments, team. Republicans are determined to hold a vote by the end of next week, and if the bill passes, millions of Americans will lose the access to affordable health care we fought so hard for under President Obama.

**We 've come too far to let all our progress slip away now. The next week is critical, so please chip in $1 right now to help fund our ads and turn the pressure up on Republicans to oppose this bill:**

[**http://dscc.org/it-is-time-to-save-obamacare**](https://secure.dscc. name: Shannon Jackson, Our Revolution
subject: Georgia on our minds
body:
Thank you for being active in this fight. This week alone, the Revolution flooded
#
Friends,
Money shouldn't be the end-all-be-all in deciding who represents us in Congress or what policies govern our country. Yet, just this week we not only finished one of the most expensive Congressional elections in history, topping out at more than $55 million, we also finally saw the Republican's "health care" bill which drops millions from our health care system to give tax breaks to the ultra wealthy.
Our Revolution groups across the country are actively fighting against the influence of money in politics. Our efforts have propelled progressive candidates backed by people, not corporate money, to victory in Philadelphia, New York, California, Iowa, and communities across the country. Together we are fighting for the issues that will benefit all, not just a lucky few. These races and issue campaigns are just the beginning of a political revolution that puts people over profits.
Now, our fight heads to the Senate as they consider a cruel plan that would kick more than 23 million people off of their current health care plans to give tax breaks to the very rich. We saw an example of this heartlessness yesterday when Capitol Police dragged away protesters in wheelchairs out of the U.S. Capitol for voicing their opposition to this cruel bill.
Thank you for being active in this fight. This week alone, the Revolution flooded the telephone lines of the Senate with more than 7,200 calls in opposition to the treacherous health care plan. But, the fight is far from over. We need your support to stop the Senate attack on health care and put our stake in the ground for Medicare for All. **[Please make a $5 contribution to Our Revolution today to help us fight back against the Republican plan to destroy our health care system.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0def/43f17890/ca08979c/620356e0/1339021371/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-healthcare?refcode=em170623-full")**
Billions of dollars from the wealthiest people in this country are flooding the political process and working Americans are losing out. It's clear that our system is broken when a health care bill would end access to maternity and mental health care but give billions to the very rich. The need for real campaign finance reform affects every aspect of our political process including how we fix our health care system.
Our Revolution is organizing in many capacities, but putting the needs of every day people will always come first. [**We need your support to fight back against the Senate's health care plan and give a voice to every day people. Please chip in $5 to Our Revolution and help us continue the political revolution together.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0def/43f17890/ca08979c/620356e0/1339021371/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-healthcare?refcode=em170623-full")
Thank you for fighting for the progressive values we all believe in. Now is the time for us to come together and stop this unjust assault on the American people.
In solidarity,
Shannon Jackson
Executive Director
Our Revolution
